sentences.quote: We report the first machine-checked development of universal quantum gate compilation as a theory rather than a single algorithm: an alphabet-agnostic, dimension-agnostic quantitative Solovay--Kitaev substrate, formally verified in the Lean~4 proof assistant, together with its instantiation across five gate alphabets and a verified resource lower bound.
sentences.quote_normalized: we report the first machine-checked development of universal quantum gate compilation as a theory rather than a single algorithm: an alphabet-agnostic, dimension-agnostic quantitative solovay--kitaev substrate, formally verified in the lean~4 proof assistant, together with its instantiation across five gate alphabets and a verified resource lower bound.
sentences.agent_notes: Self-referential framing claim ('first machine-checked development ... as a theory'). Substantiated downstream by the resolved substrate + SU(d) lift theorems; treated as metaclaim, declines hard verdict.

sentences.quote: The substrate packages the Dawson--Nielsen super-quadratic recursion, Cartan-type density-from-witness, Baker--Campbell--Hausdorff bracket closure, and the $\varepsilon_0$-net behind a generic generating-set abstraction, so that a new universal alphabet ships its verified quantitative compiler by instantiation rather than re-derivation.
sentences.quote_normalized: the substrate packages the dawson--nielsen super-quadratic recursion, cartan-type density-from-witness, baker--campbell--hausdorff bracket closure, and the $\varepsilon_0$-net behind a generic generating-set abstraction, so that a new universal alphabet ships its verified quantitative compiler by instantiation rather than re-derivation.
sentences.agent_notes: Substrate decomposition into 4 reusable ingredients; matches GenericSolovayKitaevQuantitative module family and the substrate section. Design claim resolved by existence of generic generating-set abstraction.

sentences.quote: We lift the substrate to the special unitary group $\su{d}$ of arbitrary dimension---to our knowledge the first kernel-verified quantitative Solovay--Kitaev theorem at general dimension in any proof assistant---by constructing a Banach-algebra matrix logarithm with a named convergence radius, which removes an existential obstruction that otherwise blocks the generalization.
sentences.quote_normalized: we lift the substrate to the special unitary group $\su{d}$ of arbitrary dimension---to our knowledge the first kernel-verified quantitative solovay--kitaev theorem at general dimension in any proof assistant---by constructing a banach-algebra matrix logarithm with a named convergence radius, which removes an existential obstruction that otherwise blocks the generalization.
sentences.agent_notes: SU(d) lift + matrixMercatorLog. Both resolve. 'first kernel-verified ... in any proof assistant' is a priority metaclaim; the structural part (lift theorem + named-radius log) verifies.

sentences.quote: We instantiate the machinery on Fibonacci anyons, Clifford$+T$, Read--Rezayi anyons at levels $5$ and $7$, the trapped-ion M\o lmer--S\o rensen gate set at $\su{4}$, and Clifford$+$CCZ at $\su{8}$.
sentences.quote_normalized: we instantiate the machinery on fibonacci anyons, clifford$+t$, read--rezayi anyons at levels $5$ and $7$, the trapped-ion m\o lmer--s\o rensen gate set at $\su{4}$, and clifford$+$ccz at $\su{8}$.
sentences.agent_notes: SD-check: prose 'five gate alphabets' enumerated here (Fibonacci, Clifford+T, RR k5+k7 [one family], MS SU(4), Clifford+CCZ SU(8)). Counting RR k5/k7 as the named items: Fibonacci, Clifford+T, RR(2 levels), MS, Clifford+CCZ = five alphabet entries matching the five-alphabet survey itemize (5 \item). Consistent; PASS.

sentences.quote: For the three-qubit target $\su{8}$ we give two fundamentally distinct, formally verified density mechanisms: one using the $T$ gate, and one using no $T$ gate at all, in which the doubly controlled-$Z$ (CCZ) gate is essential---accompanied by a converse proving that the Clifford group alone generates only a finite subgroup of $\su{8}$ and hence is not dense.
sentences.quote_normalized: for the three-qubit target $\su{8}$ we give two fundamentally distinct, formally verified density mechanisms: one using the $t$ gate, and one using no $t$ gate at all, in which the doubly controlled-$z$ (ccz) gate is essential---accompanied by a converse proving that the clifford group alone generates only a finite subgroup of $\su{8}$ and hence is not dense.
sentences.agent_notes: D8 honesty anchor (SU(8) duality): CCZ-essential is correctly attributed to the T-free route ONLY; converse cliffordOnly_not_dense backs finiteness. Route-A/Route-B not conflated here. Resolves.

sentences.quote: Finally we machine-check, unconditionally, a lower bound on the number of Toffoli gates required to implement a unitary, expressed through a signed-parity-invariant measure on the gate's channel representation.
sentences.quote_normalized: finally we machine-check, unconditionally, a lower bound on the number of toffoli gates required to implement a unitary, expressed through a signed-parity-invariant measure on the gate's channel representation.
sentences.agent_notes: D8 honesty anchor (unconditional Toffoli bound): channelSde2_le_toffoliCost takes only hU:IsExactlyCliffordCCZ U (no hC/hCCZ parametric hyps); verified unconditional. PASS.

sentences.quote: Every headline theorem is verified to the Lean kernel with no project-local axioms.
sentences.quote_normalized: every headline theorem is verified to the lean kernel with no project-local axioms.
sentences.agent_notes: Verified: lean_deps shows 0 declared axioms (kind=axiom); headline axiom closures = {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}, project axioms []. PASS.

sentences.quote: We delimit the scope explicitly: this is the verified existence, universality, and lower-bound theory; runtime-compiler implementation, hardware-specific tuning, and length-optimality engineering are a separate deliverable.
sentences.quote_normalized: we delimit the scope explicitly: this is the verified existence, universality, and lower-bound theory; runtime-compiler implementation, hardware-specific tuning, and length-optimality engineering are a separate deliverable.
sentences.agent_notes: Scope-delimitation metaclaim; keeps runtime/tuning/length-optimality OUT (public/private seam clean). No private-repo reference. Auto-passes.

sentences.quote: A quantum processor exposes a fixed, finite set of physical gates---an alphabet---yet must approximate an essentially continuous family of target unitaries.
sentences.quote_normalized: a quantum processor exposes a fixed, finite set of physical gates---an alphabet---yet must approximate an essentially continuous family of target unitaries.
sentences.agent_notes: Background framing; interpretive, no extractable structural claim.

sentences.quote: The Solovay--Kitaev theorem guarantees that any sufficiently rich alphabet can approximate any target to precision $\varepsilon$ using a circuit whose length grows only poly-logarithmically in $1/\varepsilon$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the solovay--kitaev theorem guarantees that any sufficiently rich alphabet can approximate any target to precision $\varepsilon$ using a circuit whose length grows only poly-logarithmically in $1/\varepsilon$.
sentences.agent_notes: Standard SK statement; cites KitaevShenVyalyi2002, DawsonNielsen2006, AharonovArad2017 — all in CITATION_REGISTRY.

sentences.quote: It is the foundational existence result behind every fault-tolerant compilation pipeline.
sentences.quote_normalized: it is the foundational existence result behind every fault-tolerant compilation pipeline.
sentences.agent_notes: Interpretive judgment; no structural claim.

sentences.quote: Its proof, however, is a delicate interplay of Lie theory (the generating set must densely fill the group), differential geometry (the balanced-commutator recursion lives on the group manifold near the identity), and number theory (density of a discrete alphabet typically rests on an irrationality or algebraic-integer obstruction).
sentences.quote_normalized: its proof, however, is a delicate interplay of lie theory (the generating set must densely fill the group), differential geometry (the balanced-commutator recursion lives on the group manifold near the identity), and number theory (density of a discrete alphabet typically rests on an irrationality or algebraic-integer obstruction).
sentences.agent_notes: Methodological exposition of SK proof structure; interpretive.

sentences.quote: This combination has made the theorem a recurring source of subtle hypotheses in the literature, and an attractive but difficult target for formal verification.
sentences.quote_normalized: this combination has made the theorem a recurring source of subtle hypotheses in the literature, and an attractive but difficult target for formal verification.
sentences.agent_notes: Interpretive motivation.

sentences.quote: This paper reports a machine-checked development, in the Lean~4 proof assistant, that treats universal compilation not as one algorithm for one alphabet but as a reusable theory.
sentences.quote_normalized: this paper reports a machine-checked development, in the lean~4 proof assistant, that treats universal compilation not as one algorithm for one alphabet but as a reusable theory.
sentences.agent_notes: Paper-scope metaclaim. Auto-passes.

sentences.quote: Our contributions are:
sentences.quote_normalized: our contributions are:
sentences.agent_notes: List-introducing transition.

sentences.quote: An alphabet-agnostic quantitative Solovay--Kitaev substrate (Sec.~\ref{sec:substrate}): the Lie-algebraic core is parametrized over an abstract generating set, so a new universal gate set inherits the full verified quantitative compiler by discharging a single density witness.
sentences.quote_normalized: an alphabet-agnostic quantitative solovay--kitaev substrate (sec.~): the lie-algebraic core is parametrized over an abstract generating set, so a new universal gate set inherits the full verified quantitative compiler by discharging a single density witness.
sentences.agent_notes: Contribution 1 = substrate; resolves to GenericSolovayKitaevQuantitative family.

sentences.quote: The lift to arbitrary dimension $\su{d}$ (Sec.~\ref{sec:sud}): the first kernel-verified quantitative Solovay--Kitaev at general $d$, enabled by a concrete-radius matrix logarithm that is a contribution to the underlying mathematical library in its own right.
sentences.quote_normalized: the lift to arbitrary dimension $\su{d}$ (sec.~): the first kernel-verified quantitative solovay--kitaev at general $d$, enabled by a concrete-radius matrix logarithm that is a contribution to the underlying mathematical library in its own right.
sentences.agent_notes: Contribution 2 = SU(d) lift; resolves (sud headline + matrixMercatorLog).

sentences.quote: A five-alphabet instantiation survey (Sec.~\ref{sec:alphabets}): Fibonacci, Clifford$+T$, Read--Rezayi $\su 2_5$ and $\su 2_7$, trapped-ion M\o lmer--S\o rensen at $\su 4$, and Clifford$+$CCZ at $\su 8$.
sentences.quote_normalized: a five-alphabet instantiation survey (sec.~): fibonacci, clifford$+t$, read--rezayi $\su 2_5$ and $\su 2_7$, trapped-ion m\o lmer--s\o rensen at $\su 4$, and clifford$+$ccz at $\su 8$.
sentences.agent_notes: SD: 'five-alphabet' matches the 5-item alphabet survey (RR k5/k7 = one Read-Rezayi family entry). Consistent with abstract enumeration.

sentences.quote: Two complementary density mechanisms for $\su 8$ (Sec.~\ref{sec:su8}): a $T$-based route and a $T$-free, CCZ-essential route, with a converse establishing that the Clifford group alone is finite in $\su 8$.
sentences.quote_normalized: two complementary density mechanisms for $\su 8$ (sec.~): a $t$-based route and a $t$-free, ccz-essential route, with a converse establishing that the clifford group alone is finite in $\su 8$.
sentences.agent_notes: Contribution 4; CCZ-essential correctly tied to T-free route. Resolves.

sentences.quote: A verified resource lower bound (Sec.~\ref{sec:resource}): an unconditional machine-checked lower bound on Toffoli count via a signed-parity-invariant measure on the channel representation, following Mukhopadhyay~\cite{Mukhopadhyay2024}.
sentences.quote_normalized: a verified resource lower bound (sec.~): an unconditional machine-checked lower bound on toffoli count via a signed-parity-invariant measure on the channel representation, following mukhopadhyay~.
sentences.agent_notes: Contribution 5; 'unconditional' verified; Mukhopadhyay2024 in registry.

sentences.quote: Coverage of the kernel-verified compilation corpus: five gate alphabets across target dimensions $\su 2$, $\su 4$, $\su 8$. Blue entries derive density from a sub-alphabet or per-qubit route; the amber entry (Phase 6z) is $T$-free with CCZ load-bearing. Every entry is verified to the Lean kernel with no project-local axioms.

sentences.agent_notes: Figure 1 caption. SD: 'five gate alphabets across SU(2),SU(4),SU(8)' consistent. Amber=Phase6z T-free CCZ-load-bearing correctly distinguished from blue sub-alphabet routes. No conflation.

sentences.quote: The quantitative Solovay--Kitaev argument has four reusable ingredients that do not depend on the specific alphabet: (i) a density statement, that the group generated by the alphabet is dense in the target group; (ii) an $\varepsilon_0$-net, a finite set of words approximating every target to a base precision $\varepsilon_0$; (iii) the Dawson--Nielsen recursion, which uses a balanced group commutator to convert a precision-$\varepsilon$ approximation into a precision-$O(\varepsilon^{3/2})$ one at controlled length cost; and (iv) a Baker--Campbell--Hausdorff (BCH) estimate controlling the commutator's deviation from the corresponding Lie-bracket flow.
sentences.quote_normalized: the quantitative solovay--kitaev argument has four reusable ingredients that do not depend on the specific alphabet: (i) a density statement, that the group generated by the alphabet is dense in the target group; (ii) an $\varepsilon_0$-net, a finite set of words approximating every target to a base precision $\varepsilon_0$; (iii) the dawson--nielsen recursion, which uses a balanced group commutator to convert a precision-$\varepsilon$ approximation into a precision-$o(\varepsilon^{3/2})$ one at controlled length cost; and (iv) a baker--campbell--hausdorff (bch) estimate controlling the commutator's deviation from the corresponding lie-bracket flow.
sentences.agent_notes: SD: 'four reusable ingredients' enumerated as (i)-(iv); matches abstract's 4-ingredient packaging. The O(eps^{3/2}) super-quadratic contraction is consistent with the honest exponent (Fig 3). PASS.

sentences.quote: We factor these into a substrate parametrized over an abstract generating set, realized in the \lean{GenericSU2GeneratingSet} and \lean{GenericSolovayKitaevQuantitative} module families.
sentences.quote_normalized: we factor these into a substrate parametrized over an abstract generating set, realized in the \lean{genericsu2generatingset} and \lean{genericsolovaykitaevquantitative} module families.
sentences.agent_notes: Module-family references: GenericSU2GeneratingSet.lean and GenericSolovayKitaevQuantitative.lean both exist under lean/SKEFTHawking/FKLW/. PASS.

sentences.quote: The headline of the substrate is a bundled-strict theorem asserting, for any generating set satisfying the density and net hypotheses, the simultaneous error bound $\lVert \mathrm{compile}(U,\varepsilon) - U\rVert \le \varepsilon$ and a poly-logarithmic word-length bound, at the same algorithmic compile level.
sentences.quote_normalized: the headline of the substrate is a bundled-strict theorem asserting, for any generating set satisfying the density and net hypotheses, the simultaneous error bound $\lvert compile(u,\varepsilon) - u\rvert \le \varepsilon$ and a poly-logarithmic word-length bound, at the same algorithmic compile level.
sentences.agent_notes: Bundled-strict substrate headline; simultaneous error+length at same compile level. Resolves to the strict_constructive_tight bundled headline.

sentences.quote: The design claim is that adding a new universal alphabet to the verified chain is an instantiation problem, not a re-derivation problem.
sentences.quote_normalized: the design claim is that adding a new universal alphabet to the verified chain is an instantiation problem, not a re-derivation problem.
sentences.agent_notes: Interpretive design claim; soft, declines hard verdict.

sentences.quote: We validate the abstraction on the canonical fault-tolerant alphabet Clifford$+T$.
sentences.quote_normalized: we validate the abstraction on the canonical fault-tolerant alphabet clifford$+t$.
sentences.agent_notes: Transition into Clifford+T instance.

sentences.quote: The density witness---that $\langle H, T\rangle$ is dense in $\su 2$---is discharged through a Niven-style algebraic-integer obstruction on the trace $\sqrt 2\,\sin(\pi/8)$ of $H_{\mathrm{SU}}\,T_{\mathrm{SU}}$ (\lean{CliffordTInfiniteOrder.lean}, theorem \lean{cliffordT\_accPt\_one\_unconditional}); this is the 1999 Boykin--Mor--Pulver--Roychowdhury--Vatan folklore result~\cite{BMPRV1999}, now machine-verified.
sentences.quote_normalized: the density witness---that $\langle h, t\rangle$ is dense in $\su 2$---is discharged through a niven-style algebraic-integer obstruction on the trace $\sqrt 2\,\sin(\pi/8)$ of $h_{su}\,t_{su}$ (\lean{cliffordtinfiniteorder.lean}, theorem \lean{cliffordt\_accpt\_one\_unconditional}); this is the 1999 boykin--mor--pulver--roychowdhury--vatan folklore result~, now machine-verified.
sentences.agent_notes: TN: cliffordT_accPt_one_unconditional resolves (module CliffordTInfiniteOrder, which exists). BMPRV1999 in registry. PASS.

sentences.quote: The result is the unconditional bundled-strict headline \lean{solovayKitaev\_dawson\_nielsen\_quantitative\_cliffordT\_strict\_constructive\_tight\_unconditional}.

sentences.quote: The algebraic-integer obstruction, rather than a cyclotomic computation, is the load-bearing novelty: it is the generic technique the remaining alphabets reuse.

sentences.quote: Multi-qubit compilation requires the same machinery at $\su d$ for $d = 2^n$.

sentences.quote: We lift all four ingredients to general $d$, yielding the headline \lean{solovayKitaev\_dawson\_nielsen\_quantitative\_generic\_sud\_strict\_constructive\_tight} (\lean{GenericSUdQuantitative.lean}): for every $d \ge 2$, every $U \in \su d$, and every $\varepsilon > 0$, a compilation exists with error $\le \varepsilon$ and poly-logarithmic length.

sentences.quote: To our knowledge this is the first kernel-verified quantitative Solovay--Kitaev at arbitrary dimension in any proof assistant.

sentences.quote: The single obstruction to the lift was the regime step: the balanced commutator construction requires writing a near-identity unitary $\Delta$ as $\exp(H)$ for a Hermitian-traceless $H$ of controlled norm, and reasoning about the round trip.
sentences.quote_normalized: the single obstruction to the lift was the regime step: the balanced commutator construction requires writing a near-identity unitary $\delta$ as $\exp(h)$ for a hermitian-traceless $h$ of controlled norm, and reasoning about the round trip.
sentences.agent_notes: Methodology exposition of the regime obstruction.

sentences.quote: The natural tool, an inverse-function-theorem matrix logarithm, provides only an existential convergence radius, which propagates a circular dependency through the recursion.
sentences.quote_normalized: the natural tool, an inverse-function-theorem matrix logarithm, provides only an existential convergence radius, which propagates a circular dependency through the recursion.
sentences.agent_notes: Methodology; explains existential-radius problem.

sentences.quote: We instead construct an explicit Banach-algebra logarithm with a named convergence radius, $\lean{matrixMercatorLog}\,X = \sum_{n\ge 0} \frac{(-1)^n}{n+1}\,X^{n+1}$, $\lVert X\rVert < 1$, and prove the round trip $\exp(\lean{matrixMercatorLog}\,X) = \mathbf 1 + X$ (\lean{GenericSUdMatrixMercatorLog.lean}, theorem \lean{exp\_matrixMercatorLog}).
sentences.quote_normalized: we instead construct an explicit banach-algebra logarithm with a named convergence radius, $\lean{matrixmercatorlog}\,x = \sum_{n\ge 0} \frac{(-1)^n}{n+1}\,x^{n+1}$, $\lvert x\rvert < 1$, and prove the round trip $\exp(\lean{matrixmercatorlog}\,x) = \mathbf 1 + x$ (\lean{genericsudmatrixmercatorlog.lean}, theorem \lean{exp\_matrixmercatorlog}).
sentences.agent_notes: TN: matrixMercatorLog + exp_matrixMercatorLog both resolve (module GenericSUdMatrixMercatorLog, exists). Series def matches source. PASS.

sentences.quote: With a concrete radius in hand, the regime conjuncts---Hermiticity, tracelessness, and the exp round trip---are all discharged unconditionally on a named ball, and the headline cascade (\lean{GenericSUdSkHeadlineCascadeConcrete.lean}) reduces to the density witness and the length bound alone.
sentences.quote_normalized: with a concrete radius in hand, the regime conjuncts---hermiticity, tracelessness, and the exp round trip---are all discharged unconditionally on a named ball, and the headline cascade (\lean{genericsudskheadlinecascadeconcrete.lean}) reduces to the density witness and the length bound alone.

sentences.quote: The matrix logarithm fills a genuine gap in the underlying library, which lacks a Banach-algebra logarithm with a named radius; we return to it in Sec.~\ref{sec:mathlib}.

sentences.quote: The Dawson--Nielsen recursion underlying the substrate: per level the error contracts super-quadratically as $\varepsilon_0^{(3/2)^n}$ while the word length grows fivefold as $N_0\,5^n$, giving the honest length exponent $\log 5/\log(3/2) \approx 3.97$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the dawson--nielsen recursion underlying the substrate: per level the error contracts super-quadratically as $\varepsilon_0^{(3/2)^n}$ while the word length grows fivefold as $n_0\,5^n$, giving the honest length exponent $\log 5/\log(3/2) \approx 3.97$.
sentences.agent_notes: Fig 3 caption. IA-check: log5/log(3/2)=3.9694 -> 3.97 (delta 0.02%). Internally consistent with body Sec length-exponent. PASS.

sentences.quote: The Dawson--Nielsen recursion contracts error as $\varepsilon \mapsto O(\varepsilon^{3/2})$ with a fivefold word-expansion per level, giving a word-length that grows as $O\!\big((\log 1/\varepsilon)^{\gamma}\big)$ with exponent $\gamma = \log 5/\log(3/2) \approx 3.97$ (Fig.~\ref{fig:recursion}).
sentences.quote_normalized: the dawson--nielsen recursion contracts error as $\varepsilon \mapsto o(\varepsilon^{3/2})$ with a fivefold word-expansion per level, giving a word-length that grows as $o\!\big((\log 1/\varepsilon)^{\gamma}\big)$ with exponent $\gamma = \log 5/\log(3/2) \approx 3.97$ (fig.~).

sentences.quote: The density witnesses require new number theory: the $k=5$ obstruction is discharged via the Chebyshev factorization $T_7(x) + 1 = (x+1)(8x^3 - 4x^2 - 4x + 1)^2$ (forcing the level-5 cubic at $\cos(\pi/7)$), and the $k=7$ obstruction via the triple-angle identity $\cos(3\cdot\pi/9) = \cos(\pi/3) = 1/2$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the density witnesses require new number theory: the $k=5$ obstruction is discharged via the chebyshev factorization $t_7(x) + 1 = (x+1)(8x^3 - 4x^2 - 4x + 1)^2$ (forcing the level-5 cubic at $\cos(\pi/7)$), and the $k=7$ obstruction via the triple-angle identity $\cos(3\cdot\pi/9) = \cos(\pi/3) = 1/2$.
sentences.agent_notes: IA: Chebyshev identity T_7(x)+1=(x+1)(8x^3-4x^2-4x+1)^2 verified exactly by polynomial recomputation (residual=0). cos(3*pi/9)=cos(pi/3)=0.5 verified. Both arithmetic identities correct.

Density via the Brylinski--Brylinski entangling-gate criterion~\cite{Brylinski2002}, giving the unconditional headline \lean{trappedIonSU4\_solovayKitaev\_headline\_unconditional} (\lean{TrappedIonSU4WitnessFull.lean}).

sentences.quote: Clifford$+$CCZ ($\su 8$): two distinct routes, treated in Sec.~\ref{sec:su8}.
sentences.quote_normalized: clifford$+$ccz ($\su 8$): two distinct routes, treated in sec.~.

sentences.quote: The three-qubit target $\su 8$ admits two formally verified density mechanisms that are fundamentally different, and we present both because their contrast is itself the result (Fig.~\ref{fig:su8duality}).
sentences.quote_normalized: the three-qubit target $\su 8$ admits two formally verified density mechanisms that are fundamentally different, and we present both because their contrast is itself the result (fig.~).
sentences.agent_notes: Frames the two-route duality. Backed downstream. PASS.

sentences.quote: Route A: $T$-based, CCZ over-complete. Using the universal alphabet $\{H, T, \mathrm{CNOT}, \mathrm{CCZ}\}$, density follows entirely from the $\{H, T, \mathrm{CNOT}\}$ sub-alphabet, in the manner of Aaronson--Gottesman~\cite{AaronsonGottesman2004}; CCZ is present but over-complete (unused in the witness).
sentences.quote_normalized: route a: $t$-based, ccz over-complete. using the universal alphabet $\{h, t, cnot, ccz\}$, density follows entirely from the $\{h, t, cnot\}$ sub-alphabet, in the manner of aaronson--gottesman~; ccz is present but over-complete (unused in the witness).
sentences.agent_notes: D8 honesty anchor: Route A correctly states CCZ over-complete / not load-bearing; density from {H,T,CNOT} sub-alphabet (Aaronson-Gottesman). AaronsonGottesman2004 in registry. No conflation with Route B. PASS.

sentences.quote: Route B: $T$-free, CCZ-essential. Using the literal alphabet $\langle H, S, \mathrm{CNOT}, \mathrm{CCZ}\rangle$ with no $T$ gate, we prove density directly: \lean{cliffordCCZLiteral\_dense} (\lean{CliffordCCZSU8LiteralHeadline.lean}).

Symbol resolves so not a hard TN FAIL; flagged WARN for module-attribution drift. Route-B CCZ-essential claim itself is correct.

sentences.quote: The mechanism is distinct from Route~A. Density does not come from a per-qubit infinite-order element; it comes from the seed $\mathrm{CCZ}\cdot(H\otimes H\otimes H)$, whose trace $1/\sqrt 2 \notin \mathcal O$ (an algebraic-integer obstruction) forces infinite order (\lean{CliffordCCZSU8LiteralSeed.lean}).
sentences.quote_normalized: the mechanism is distinct from route~a. density does not come from a per-qubit infinite-order element; it comes from the seed $ccz\cdot(h\otimes h\otimes h)$, whose trace $1/\sqrt 2 \notin \mathcal o$ (an algebraic-integer obstruction) forces infinite order (\lean{cliffordcczsu8literalseed.lean}).
sentences.agent_notes: TN: module CliffordCCZSU8LiteralSeed.lean exists. Seed trace 1/sqrt2 not an algebraic integer -> infinite order; mechanism distinct from Route A. PASS.

sentences.quote: A one-parameter flow accumulated from the seed is then spread across the full Lie algebra $\mathfrak{su}(8)$ by Clifford-conjugation irreducibility, the Clifford group acting transitively on the $63$ non-identity Pauli lines.
sentences.quote_normalized: a one-parameter flow accumulated from the seed is then spread across the full lie algebra $\mathfrak{su}(8)$ by clifford-conjugation irreducibility, the clifford group acting transitively on the $63$ non-identity pauli lines.
sentences.agent_notes: IA: '63 non-identity Pauli lines' for 3 qubits = 4^3 - 1 = 63. Correct. Density-spreading mechanism narrative.

sentences.agent_notes: TN: cliffordCCZLiteral_solovayKitaev_headline_unconditional resolves (module CliffordCCZSU8LiteralHeadline). PASS.

sentences.quote: CCZ is essential. Route~B is complemented by a converse (Sec.~\ref{sec:resource}): the Clifford group alone, $\langle H, S, \mathrm{CNOT}\rangle$, generates a finite subgroup of $\su 8$ and is therefore not dense.
sentences.quote_normalized: ccz is essential. route~b is complemented by a converse (sec.~): the clifford group alone, $\langle h, s, cnot\rangle$, generates a finite subgroup of $\su 8$ and is therefore not dense.
sentences.agent_notes: D8 honesty anchor: CCZ-essentiality backed by cliffordOnly_not_dense converse. Correctly scoped to Route B. PASS.

sentences.quote: CCZ is the unique resource whose addition makes the difference. The pairing of Routes~A and~B settles the question of why two $\su 8$ density proofs coexist: they rest on genuinely distinct mechanisms (a per-qubit $T$ route and a global CCZ-seed route), not on redundancy or over-generality.
sentences.quote_normalized: ccz is the unique resource whose addition makes the difference. the pairing of routes~a and~b settles the question of why two $\su 8$ density proofs coexist: they rest on genuinely distinct mechanisms (a per-qubit $t$ route and a global ccz-seed route), not on redundancy or over-generality.
sentences.agent_notes: Interpretive synthesis of the two-route duality; soft claim, structurally backed by the two headlines + converse.

sentences.quote: Two complementary, formally verified density mechanisms for the same target $\su 8$. Route~A (Phase 6y) is $T$-based with CCZ over-complete; Route~B (Phase 6z) is $T$-free with CCZ essential. The converse (Phase 6x$'$, $\lean{cliffordOnly\_not\_dense}$) establishes that the Clifford group alone is finite, so CCZ is the unique resource that makes the difference.
sentences.quote_normalized: two complementary, formally verified density mechanisms for the same target $\su 8$. route~a (phase 6y) is $t$-based with ccz over-complete; route~b (phase 6z) is $t$-free with ccz essential. the converse (phase 6x$'$, $\lean{cliffordonly\_not\_dense}$) establishes that the clifford group alone is finite, so ccz is the unique resource that makes the difference.
sentences.agent_notes: Fig 2 caption. D8 honesty anchor: Route A 6y over-complete vs Route B 6z essential correctly distinguished; converse cliffordOnly_not_dense resolves. No conflation. PASS.

sentences.quote: Compilation existence has a dual: how cheaply can a target be built? We formalize the channel-representation resource theory of Mukhopadhyay~\cite{Mukhopadhyay2024} for Clifford$+$CCZ in $\su 8$ and machine-check, unconditionally, a Toffoli-count lower bound.
sentences.quote_normalized: compilation existence has a dual: how cheaply can a target be built? we formalize the channel-representation resource theory of mukhopadhyay~ for clifford$+$ccz in $\su 8$ and machine-check, unconditionally, a toffoli-count lower bound.
sentences.agent_notes: Mukhopadhyay2024 in registry. 'unconditionally' verified. PASS.

sentences.quote: The central object is a signed-parity-invariant measure $\mathrm{sde}_2$ on the channel representation $\widehat U$ of a unitary $U$. We prove that this measure is non-increasing under Clifford multiplication and increases by at most one under a CCZ (equivalently Toffoli) gate; the channel-representation entries of any Clifford$+$CCZ word are dyadic, lying in $\mathbb Z[1/2]$ (Lemma~3.10, \lean{channelRep\_interp\_isRat}).
sentences.quote_normalized: the central object is a signed-parity-invariant measure $sde_2$ on the channel representation $\widehat u$ of a unitary $u$. we prove that this measure is non-increasing under clifford multiplication and increases by at most one under a ccz (equivalently toffoli) gate; the channel-representation entries of any clifford$+$ccz word are dyadic, lying in $\mathbb z[1/2]$ (lemma~3.10, \lean{channelrep\_interp\_israt}).
sentences.agent_notes: TN: channelRep_interp_isRat resolves (SKEFTHawking.FKLW.MukhopadhyayCCZ.channelRep_interp_isRat, module MukhopadhyayToffoliUnconditional). Dyadic Z[1/2] / Lemma 3.10 narrative matches. PASS.

sentences.quote: Telescoping these facts gives the lower bound $T^{\mathrm{of}}(U) \;\ge\; \mathrm{sde}_2(\widehat U)$, formalized as \lean{channelSde2\_le\_toffoliCost} (\lean{MukhopadhyayToffoliUnconditional.lean}).
sentences.quote_normalized: telescoping these facts gives the lower bound $t^{of}(u) \;\ge\; sde_2(\widehat u)$, formalized as \lean{channelsde2\_le\_toffolicost} (\lean{mukhopadhyaytoffoliunconditional.lean}).
sentences.agent_notes: D8 honesty anchor: channelSde2_le_toffoliCost resolves (module MukhopadhyayToffoliUnconditional, exists). Statement T^of(U) >= sde2(U-hat) matches source (channelSde2 U <= toffoliCost U). PASS.

sentences.quote: The earlier form of this bound was parametric (conditional on the Clifford-preservation and CCZ-increment hypotheses); we discharge those hypotheses and obtain the unconditional statement.
sentences.quote_normalized: the earlier form of this bound was parametric (conditional on the clifford-preservation and ccz-increment hypotheses); we discharge those hypotheses and obtain the unconditional statement.
sentences.agent_notes: D8 honesty anchor (unconditional vs parametric): confirmed against source — channelSde2_le_toffoliCost takes only hU:IsExactlyCliffordCCZ U; hC/hCCZ discharged. Statement of provenance is accurate. PASS.

sentences.quote: A fully machine-checked unconditional resource lower bound for a fault-tolerant gate is, to our knowledge, the first of its kind and is directly usable in magic-state-distillation resource accounting.
sentences.quote_normalized: a fully machine-checked unconditional resource lower bound for a fault-tolerant gate is, to our knowledge, the first of its kind and is directly usable in magic-state-distillation resource accounting.
sentences.agent_notes: First-of-kind priority metaclaim; usability assertion interpretive. Declines hard verdict.

sentences.quote: The converse of Sec.~\ref{sec:su8} is the same machinery read in the density direction: the channel representation maps the Clifford group into signed-permutation matrices, a finite group, giving \lean{cliffordOnly\_not\_dense} (\lean{MukhopadhyayCliffordNotDense.lean}).
sentences.quote_normalized: the converse of sec.~ is the same machinery read in the density direction: the channel representation maps the clifford group into signed-permutation matrices, a finite group, giving \lean{cliffordonly\_not\_dense} (\lean{mukhopadhyaycliffordnotdense.lean}).
sentences.agent_notes: TN: cliffordOnly_not_dense resolves (module MukhopadhyayCliffordNotDense, exists). Signed-permutation finite-group mechanism matches. PASS.

sentences.quote: Several components of this development are general-purpose and are candidates for upstream contribution to the underlying mathematical library: the concrete-radius matrix logarithm of Sec.~\ref{sec:sud} (the strongest standalone candidate, as the library lacks a Banach-algebra logarithm with a named convergence radius); a dimension-generic BCH order-two cubic estimate; the $\su d$ Cartan density-from-witness lemma; and the matrix-exponential local-homeomorphism at the identity.

sentences.quote: We state the boundary of this work explicitly, both to forestall over-reading and because the boundary is itself a deliberate design choice.

sentences.quote: This paper establishes the verified existence, universality, and lower-bound theory of universal quantum gate compilation: that a compilation exists, with provable error and word-length bounds, at arbitrary dimension, for the alphabets treated here, and that the associated resource costs obey machine-checked bounds.
sentences.quote_normalized: this paper establishes the verified existence, universality, and lower-bound theory of universal quantum gate compilation: that a compilation exists, with provable error and word-length bounds, at arbitrary dimension, for the alphabets treated here, and that the associated resource costs obey machine-checked bounds.
sentences.agent_notes: Scope summary metaclaim; backed by headlines. Auto-passes.

sentences.quote: What is not in scope is the engineering of a runnable compiler: the runtime gate-word-emitting implementation, hardware-specific tuning for particular devices, certificate formats, and length-optimality (the gap between our poly-logarithmic guarantee and the optimal Ross--Selinger-style synthesis~\cite{RossSelinger2016}) are a separate engineering deliverable.
sentences.quote_normalized: what is not in scope is the engineering of a runnable compiler: the runtime gate-word-emitting implementation, hardware-specific tuning for particular devices, certificate formats, and length-optimality (the gap between our poly-logarithmic guarantee and the optimal ross--selinger-style synthesis~) are a separate engineering deliverable.
sentences.agent_notes: D8 public/private seam honesty anchor: runtime impl, vendor tuning, cert formats, length-optimality all explicitly OUT. No private-repo reference. RossSelinger2016 in registry. PASS.

sentences.quote: The existential $\varepsilon_0$-net used here can in principle be replaced by a genuinely runnable algorithmic synthesis following Kliuchnikov--Maslov--Mosca~\cite{KMM2013} and Ross--Selinger~\cite{RossSelinger2016} over the ring $\mathbb Z[\omega][1/\sqrt 2]$; that refinement is recorded as future work.
sentences.quote_normalized: the existential $\varepsilon_0$-net used here can in principle be replaced by a genuinely runnable algorithmic synthesis following kliuchnikov--maslov--mosca~ and ross--selinger~ over the ring $\mathbb z[\omega][1/\sqrt 2]$; that refinement is recorded as future work.
sentences.agent_notes: Future-work pointer; KMM2013 + RossSelinger2016 both in registry. Z[omega][1/sqrt2] is the standard KMM ring. PASS.

sentences.quote: The development demonstrates a methodology as much as a result: by factoring the alphabet-specific from the alphabet-agnostic and lifting the latter to arbitrary dimension, the cost of verifying a new universal gate set is reduced to discharging a single density witness, and the verified theory now spans dimensions $2$, $4$, and $8$ and five physically distinct alphabets.
sentences.quote_normalized: the development demonstrates a methodology as much as a result: by factoring the alphabet-specific from the alphabet-agnostic and lifting the latter to arbitrary dimension, the cost of verifying a new universal gate set is reduced to discharging a single density witness, and the verified theory now spans dimensions $2$, $4$, and $8$ and five physically distinct alphabets.
sentences.agent_notes: SD: 'dimensions 2,4,8' + 'five physically distinct alphabets' consistent with the survey. PASS.

sentences.quote: The two-mechanism $\su 8$ result and the unconditional Toffoli bound suggest that formal verification is reaching the point where it can serve not only as a correctness check on known compilation results but as a tool for settling structural questions---such as the essentiality of a gate---that are easy to state and surprisingly delicate to prove.
sentences.quote_normalized: the two-mechanism $\su 8$ result and the unconditional toffoli bound suggest that formal verification is reaching the point where it can serve not only as a correctness check on known compilation results but as a tool for settling structural questions---such as the essentiality of a gate---that are easy to state and surprisingly delicate to prove.
sentences.agent_notes: Interpretive closing reflection.

sentences.quote: Relationship to companion work. The Fibonacci-universality and topological/categorical foundations are developed in a companion bundle, which this paper cites for the Fibonacci anchor and generalizes; the fault-tolerant-computation substrate (codes, measurement, gauging) is developed in a sibling bundle that consumes the quantitative Solovay--Kitaev developed here as its universal-compilation primitive.
